seoTitle: Delete Your QuickBooks Online Data and Start Over
metaDescription: Learn how to delete your QuickBooks Online data and start fresh, useful when switching platforms or restructuring your books.
intro: Sometimes a clean slate is the best way forward, whether you're switching accounting platforms or restructuring after a rough quarter. This guide covers how to navigate QuickBooks Online's settings to delete your data and start over.

steps.heading: Access QuickBooks Online settings
steps.body: Open the settings menu where account and data management options are located.

steps.heading: Navigate to data deletion
steps.body: Locate the option for purging or deleting company data, which is typically found within account or company settings.

steps.heading: Confirm the deletion
steps.body: Review the warning prompts carefully, since this action is generally irreversible, and confirm once you're certain.

steps.heading: Plan your next steps
steps.body: After deletion, set up your company file fresh, importing any data you want to retain from backups if needed.

faq.question: Can I undo a data deletion in QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: No, deleting company data is generally permanent, so it's important to back up anything you might need before proceeding.

faq.question: Why would a business want to start over?
faq.answer: Common reasons include switching accounting methods, cleaning up years of messy data, or preparing for a major restructuring.

faq.question: Is there a time limit for purging data?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online typically allows data purging only within a certain window after account creation, so check your eligibility before starting.

faq.question: Should I consult an accountant before deleting data?
faq.answer: Yes, given the permanence of this action, it's wise to consult your accountant to ensure you're not losing records needed for compliance.
